toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try downloading the libmonosgen-2.0.so binary from Microsoft symbol server using BuildId
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
mkdir -p /tmp/symbols

# Try ELF buildid format
curl -sSL -o /tmp/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" \
  -w "\nHTTP_CODE: %{http_code}\nSIZE: %{size_download}\n" 2>&1
arguments.description: Download mono runtime binary from symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download mono runtime binary from symbol server

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Download debug symbols from symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download debug symbols from symbol server

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in this tombstone. The key frames are from `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`. 

To symbolicate these, I would need:
1. The debug symbols for that specific build of `libmonosgen-2.0.so`
2. A tool like `llvm-symbolizer`, `addr2line`, or similar to map the PC offsets to function names

The BuildId is `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`. This is the Mono runtime library used by .NET MAUI on Android.


I don't have access to the actual binary or debug symbols, but Microsoft publishes these symbols on their symbol server. I can try fetching them using the BuildId through Microsoft's symbol server at `https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/`, which uses the BuildId to locate the correct symbols for this specific runtime build. Constructing the full symbol server URLs for both the library and debug info, then I'll try downloading them and using addr2line or llvm-symbolizer to resolve the stack addresses.

arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5

# Try downloading the libmonosgen-2.0.so binary from Microsoft symbol server using BuildId
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
mkdir -p /tmp/symbols

# Try ELF buildid format
curl -sSL -o /tmp/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" \
  -w "\nHTTP_CODE: %{http_code}\nSIZE: %{size_download}\n" 2>&1

Download debug symbols from symbol server

arguments.command:
# Symbolicate all libmonosgen-2.0.so frames using the debug symbols
# PC offsets from the tombstone
ADDRS="0x222098 0x2241ac 0x1abc80 0x1abd14 0x16dfa4 0x16db6c 0x16de04"
for addr in $ADDRS; do
  echo "=== $addr ==="
  llvm-addr2line -e /tmp/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so.dbg -f -C "$addr" 2>&1
  echo ""
done

data.reasoningText:

data.content:
Here's the symbolicated backtrace for your .NET MAUI crash:

```
#00 pc 0x06ae1c  libc.so                (abort+164)
#01 pc 0x222098  libmonosgen-2.0.so     ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast        (icall.c:6244)
#02 pc 0x2241ac  libmonosgen-2.0.so     ves_icall_System_Environment_FailFast_raw    (icall-def.h:202)
#03 pc 0x1abc80  libmonosgen-2.0.so     do_icall                                     (interp.c:2457)
#04 pc 0x1abd14  libmonosgen-2.0.so     get_interp_to_native_trampoline              (interp.c:1732)
#05 pc 0x16dfa4  libmonosgen-2.0.so     handle_isinst                                (type-checking.c:0)
#06 pc 0x16db6c  libmonosgen-2.0.so     handle_isinst                                (type-checking.c:506)
#07 pc 0x16de04  libmonosgen-2.0.so     handle_isinst                                (type-checking.c:528)
#08 pc 0x0000307c  <anonymous>          (managed/JIT code)
```

**Summary:** The crash is a `SIGABRT` triggered by `Environment.FailFast()` with the message *"not combatible"*. The call originates from a type-checking path (`handle_isinst` — the `isinst` IL instruction) in the Mono interpreter, suggesting a type compatibility check failed during startup. Frame `#08` is JIT'd managed code (not symbolicated from native symbols — you'd need a managed stack trace or `dotnet-dsrouter` for that).

Debug symbols were fetched from Microsoft's symbol server using BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`.
